title: Dog Excessive Sleeping After Vaccines: Is It Normal?
meta_desc: Learn why dogs sleep more after vaccinations, what to expect during recovery, and how to tell if your pet's lethargy is a cause for concern.
content_html:
TL;DR: It is normal for dogs to be lethargic for 24–48 hours after a vaccine while their immune system builds defenses, and it is rarely an emergency unless they cannot be easily woken.

Why is my dog so tired after receiving routine vaccinations?

It is very common for dogs to experience a period of lethargy or excessive sleeping after receiving their routine vaccinations. This occurs because the vaccine is designed to stimulate the immune system, prompting the body to build defenses against specific diseases. This process requires significant energy, which often leaves your dog feeling tired or "under the weather" for a day or two.

Is it an emergency if my dog is sleeping excessively after vaccinations?

  • Recognize that in the vast majority of cases, post-vaccine lethargy is not an emergency and the urgency level is considered low.
  • Monitor your dog for 24 to 48 hours, as most pets return to their normal selves within this timeframe.
  • Ensure your dog can be easily woken up and is still willing to drink water.
  • Allow your pet to rest, as sleep is often the best medicine during this immune response.

How can I use photos and videos to help my vet evaluate my dog's lethargy?

  • Take a photo of the injection site to help the vet check for abnormal swelling or redness.
  • Record a short video of your dog's breathing and responsiveness to help a professional determine if the lethargy is within a normal range.
  • Submit this visual evidence to your veterinarian to allow for a much faster and more accurate remote assessment.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Vaccines can cause adverse effects, with smaller dogs being more susceptible due to receiving a relatively larger dose. The stress from a vaccination reaction may activate latent infections.

Chapter: Immunology, Infectious Diseases, Reproduction, Pharmacology, Preventive Medicine

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 2773)

faqs.question: How long will my dog be sleepy after vaccines?
faqs.answer: Most dogs experience mild lethargy for about 24 to 48 hours after vaccination. If the sleepiness persists beyond two days, you should contact your veterinarian.

faqs.question: What are other normal side effects?
faqs.answer: In addition to sleeping more, your dog may have a slightly decreased appetite or a mild fever. You might also notice a small, firm lump at the site where the injection was given.

faqs.question: When should I call an emergency vet?
faqs.answer: While sleepiness is normal, you should seek immediate care if your dog experiences facial swelling, hives, persistent vomiting, or difficulty breathing, as these can be signs of a rare allergic reaction.
